question.content: In the figure, AB is a diameter of circle O, point C lies on circle O, and segment AC is drawn. Construct rhombus ACDE with side AC. Line CD intersects circle O again at point F, and AB is perpendicular to CD with foot G. Connect AD, which intersects circle O again at point H, and connect EH. Given AG=12 and GF=5, find the length of DF. Answer format requirement: <expr>. Strictly forbidden: any extra text beyond this format.

step_1.content: Set G as origin with AB vertical and CD horizontal. From AG=12 and GF=5, take A(0,12), G(0,0), F(5,0), and by circle symmetry C(-5,0).
step_1.key_point: AB is a diameter line through center O, so C and F are symmetric on CD.

step_3.content: Compute AC=sqrt((12)^2+(5)^2)=13. Since ACDE is a rhombus, CD=AC=13, and C,F,D are collinear on CD.
step_3.key_point: CF=10 from C(-5,0) to F(5,0), then DF=CD-CF.

step_5.key_point: Subtract the known chord segment CF from rhombus side CD.
